articles.description: Israel launched a daylight attack Saturday on Iran's capital, with a cloud of smoke rising from the city's downtown near the offices of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.title: Live updates: Israel confirms attack on Iran as explosions are heard in Tehran

articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel struck Iran on Saturday. President Donald Trump said Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead, telling Iranians to take back their country.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump says Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is dead. Iran has not confirmed his death

articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel struck Iran on Saturday. President Donald Trump said Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead, telling Iranians to take back their country.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump says Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is dead. Iran has not confirmed his death

articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel struck Iran on Saturday. President Donald Trump said Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead, telling Iranians to take back their country.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump says Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is dead. Iran has not confirmed his death

articles.author: Brian Melley
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel have launched major attacks on Iran that President Donald Trump says aim to crush Iran’s military and stop production of a nuclear weapon. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it fired drones and missiles at Israel and strikes aimed at U.S. military installations in Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar. Several countries closed their airspace as explosions were heard across the region. Smoke was seen rising in Tehran near offices tied to Iran’s supreme leader. Officials report little damage in Israel and no known injuries. Trump posted a video urging Iranians to take cover and later rise up and take control of their government.
articles.title: US and Israel launch major attack on Iran amid nuclear tensions

articles.author: Suman Naishadham, Sam Metz
articles.description: After Saturday's major attack on Iran by the U.S. and Israel, U.S. President Donald Trump has called on the Iranian public to “seize control of your destiny” and rise up against the Islamic leadership that has ruled the nation since 1979. Israeli officials told The Associated Press that Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the attack. The two Israeli officials spoke on condition of anonymity pending a formal announcement and gave no further details. Trump announced the death soon after on social media, saying “the single greatest chance for the Iranian people to take back their Country” had arrived with Khamenei’s death. Yet even if Iran’s top leaders are killed, regime change is not guaranteed.
articles.title: Key political and religious figures in Iran

articles.author: Cara Rubinsky, Marc Levy, Josh Funk
articles.description: America and Israel’s attack on Iran disrupted flights across the Middle East and beyond Saturday as countries around the region closed their airspace and three of the key airports that connect Europe, Africa and the West to Asia halted operations. Hundreds of thousands of travelers were either stranded or diverted to other airports after Israel, Qatar, Syria, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait and Bahrain closed their airspace. Flights also stopped over the United Arab Emirates. That led to the closure of key hub airports in Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Doha, and the cancellation of more than 1,800 flights by major Middle Eastern airlines. Airlines are telling passengers to check status online and may offer waivers to help travelers rebook their flights if needed.
articles.title: Military strikes on Iran disrupt airline flights

articles.description: World leaders are responding cautiously after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran raised fears of a wider war.
articles.title: World leaders react to US and Israeli strikes on Iran as fears grow of a wider war

articles.description: The U.S. Central Command has released videos showing missiles being fired and jets taking off in what it said was strikes "to dismantle the Iranian regime’s security apparatus".
articles.title: US military releases videos said to show strikes on Iran being launched

articles.description: Secretary-General Antonio Guterres told an emergency meeting of the U.N. Security Council that U.S. and Israeli airstrikes reportedly killed Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei.
articles.title: UN chief shares report that U.S. and Israeli strikes killed Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei
